perturbation.original_text: Tickets agrees to pay MP3.com, during the term of this Agreement, as follows: (i) $[***] payable on the Effective Date; (ii) $[***] payable on or before one month subsequent to the Effective Date; (iii) $[***] payable on or before two months subsequent to the Effective Date; (iv) $[***] payable on or before three months subsequent to the Effective Date; (v) $[***] payable on or before four months subsequent to the Effective Date; (vi) $[***] payable on or before five months subsequent to the Effective Date. Any late payments under this Agreement will be assessed a service fee of one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month, to the extent allowed by law.
perturbation.changed_text: Tickets agrees to remit to MP3.com, during the term of this Agreement, as follows: (i) $[***] payable on the Effective Date; (ii) $[***] payable on or before one month subsequent to the Effective Date; (iii) $[***] payable on or before two months subsequent to the Effective Date; (iv) $[***] payable on or before three months subsequent to the Effective Date; (v) $[***] payable on or before four months subsequent to the Effective Date; (vi) $[***] payable on or before five months subsequent to the Effective Date. Any late remittances under this Agreement will be assessed a service fee of two percent (2%) per month, to the extent allowed by law.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'pay' has been replaced with 'remit'. Also, the 'service fee' changed from 'one and one-half percent (1.5%)' to 'two percent (2%)'. This creates an in-text contradiction by defining the payment obligation with two different standards, making it unclear which late fee applies. Is it 'pay' or is it 'remit' and is it '1.5%' or '2%'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sponsor Fees. Tickets agrees to remit to MP3.com, during the term of this Agreement, as follows: (i) $[***] payable on the Effective Date; (ii) $[***] payable on or before one month subsequent to the Effective Date; (iii) $[***] payable on or before two months subsequent to the Effective Date; (iv) $[***] payable on or before three months subsequent to the Effective Date; (v) $[***] payable on or before four months subsequent to the Effective Date; (vi) $[***] payable on or before five months subsequent to the Effective Date. Any late remittances under this Agreement will be assessed a service fee of two percent (2%) per month, to the extent allowed by law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the late payment fee from 1.5% to 2%. Since the contradicted text also uses 'remit' and states that the late remittance fee is 2%, this creates a contradiction. If the original text had not been changed, then the late payment fee would have been 1.5% as according to the orignal agreement. The change introduces an ambiguity. Is the late fee 1.5% or 2%? This creates a direct conflict within the document regarding the applicable late payment fee.

perturbation.original_text: MP3.com agrees to deliver a guaranteed minimum of 3,000,000 Impressions per month for the term of this Agreement. An "Impression" is defined as the display of the Tickets Portal to a user on one of the above referenced pages.
perturbation.changed_text: MP3.com agrees to provide a targeted minimum of 3,000,000 Views per month for the term of this Agreement. A "View" is defined as the display of the Tickets Portal to a user on one of the above referenced pages, where the user actively clicks on the displayed portal.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Impression' has been replaced with 'View', and the definition of the metric now requires a user click, which was not originally required. This contradicts the intended performance obligations, as one term implies simple display, while the other implies user engagement (click).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon request, Tickets shall have access to pertinent statistics related to Impressions covering the period of this contract. Tickets agrees to accept MP3.com's measurement of Impressions (the "Count") according to MP3.com's logs and other tracking devices and/or software MP3.com may use, provided however, that Tickets shall have the right to audit MP3.com's records in this regard. If Tickets reasonably disputes the Count pursuant to this Agreement, then Tickets shall have the right to select the independent auditor of its choice to conduct an audit of MP3.com's records (the "Audit"). The Audit will be conducted in such a way so as not to interfere to any material extent with MP3.com's operations. If, for any applicable period, the independent auditor determines that MP3.com overstated the Count by more than five percent (5%), than MP3.com shall pay the cost of the Audit and shall refund Tickets the difference between the amount originally paid and the amount which should have been paid, or MP3.com shall credit the appropriate amount of Impressions to Tickets' account. [***] Confidential treatment has been requested for redacted portion. The confidential redacted portion has been omitted and filed separately with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Impressions" to "Views" fundamentally alters the metric being measured. The original text defines 'Impression' as a simple display, while the changed text defines 'View' as a display with a user click. The contradicted text explicitly refers to 'Impressions' and MP3.com's measurement of 'Impressions'. Because the changed text replaces 'Impressions' with 'Views', the contradicted text now refers to a metric that no longer exists as defined in the agreement, creating a direct contradiction regarding how performance is measured and audited.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by Tickets without MP3.com's written consent, which shall be promptly granted or denied and not unreasonably withheld, except that Tickets may assign this Agreement without MP3.com's consent if another entity acquires substantially all the assets of Tickets.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may not be transferred by Tickets without MP3.com's explicit consent, which shall be promptly granted or denied and not unreasonably withheld, except that Tickets may transfer this Agreement without MP3.com's consent if another entity acquires the majority ownership of Tickets.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'assigned' has been replaced with 'transferred', and the condition for automatic transfer changed from 'acquires substantially all the assets' to 'acquires the majority ownership'. This introduces ambiguity regarding the required level of asset acquisition for an automatic transfer/assignment of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California without reference to conflict of law principles thereof. Any claim arising out of or related to this Agreement must be brought exclusively in the state or federal courts located in San Diego County, California, and each party hereby consent to the jurisdiction thereof. In any action to enforce this Agreement the prevailing party will be entitled to costs and attorneys' fees. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior discussions, documents, agreements and prior course of dealing, and shall not be effective until signed by both parties. This Agreement may not be transferred by Tickets without MP3.com's explicit consent, which shall be promptly granted or denied and not unreasonably withheld, except that Tickets may transfer this Agreement without MP3.com's consent if another entity acquires the majority ownership of Tickets. The parties to this Agreement are independent contractors, and no agency, partnership, joint venture or employee-employer relationship is created by this Agreement. MP3.com intends to, and does, bind its successors and assigns to the terms of this Agreement. /s/ Greg Flores /s/ Adam Epstein - ----------------------------------- ----------------------------------- Representative of MP3.com Representative of Tickets.com, Inc. /s/ Greg Flores, VP Sales Adam Epstein, SVP Counsel - ----------------------------------- ----------------------------------- Printed Name & Position Printed Name & Position - ----------------------------------- ----------------------------------- Date Date
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'assigned' with 'transferred' and alters the condition for automatic transfer from 'acquires substantially all the assets' to 'acquires the majority ownership'. Since both the changed text and contradicted text are in the same clause, this creates a direct in-text contradiction within the contract itself. The contradiction arises because the condition for automatic transfer/assignment has been altered, introducing ambiguity regarding the required level of asset acquisition, which is an example of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'. This directly impacts the enforecability and understanding of this clause, and so falls under an in-text contradiction.
